title: WIP: DO NOT MERGE: Abusing CI system - Add a task that always happens last

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **NOT APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *cevich* To complete the [pull request process](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process), please assign **vrothberg** You can assign the PR to them by writing `/assign @vrothberg` in a comment when ready. The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod).
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - **[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)** Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably #5344) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.

@edsantiago PTAL at this idea.  Unfortunately the new Mergify app. only responds to it's configuration on the target branch (`master` in this case).  Fortunately, they [do provide a pull-request simulator](https://dashboard.mergify.io/installation/7141672/simulator).  So I may need to create a dummy/test repository if we really want to fully test out your log-annotation summarizing script (I'm okay with doing this). 

The catch is, we've got to rename all/most of the Cirrus-CI tasks to include an easily matched prefix.  For example `testing_task` ->  `cirrus-ci/required/testing_task`. I had to do this when enabling bors-ng on buildah for the same reason:  The tool needs to be able to distinguish among multiple automation systems using only the status name.  Grasping the `.mergify.yml` file I added (in this PR) will make this more clear...
Gahhhh....nevermind @edsantiago this won't work.  Trying something simpler instead.

Won't work reason:  In order to post a link to a generated summary of test failures, the text must be specific to the PR/branch it relates to.  However, Mergify only operates based on the target branch's copy of `.mergify.yml` - where the text to post is defined.   I could not find any way in their documentation, such as using substitution tokens in the text, to workaround this.
